description:
Week-4 of the HCL Java CDC program covers Database Management.

Sections:
1. Databases — MySQL/SQL (10 Hours)
2. NO-SQL — MongoDB (12 Hours)
3. Authentication & Authorization — JWT (5 Hours)
4. Unit Testing — JUnit + Mockito (5 Hours)

Faculty:
• SQL: Dr. JENILA LIVINGSTON L M — jenila.lm@vit.ac.in
• MongoDB: Dr. LENINISHA SHANMUGAM
• JWT: Dr. MANSOOR HUSSAIN D
• Unit Testing: Dr. ALOK CHAUHAN

Goal: Build and query relational and NoSQL databases, implement JWT-secured APIs, write proper unit tests.

Instructor-led session — Duration: 10 Hours
Faculty: Dr. JENILA LIVINGSTON L M

Relational databases store data in tables (rows and columns) with relationships.
MySQL is an open-source RDBMS — most popular for Spring Boot applications.

MYSQL ENGINES:
  InnoDB (default) — ACID-compliant, foreign keys, transactions, row-level locking
  MyISAM — faster reads, no transactions, table-level locking (legacy)
  MEMORY — in-memory, fastest, data lost on restart

Topics:
• Basic queries — CREATE DATABASE, CREATE TABLE, INSERT, UPDATE, ALTER
• SELECT query & operations
• COUNT & SUM
• UPDATE & DELETE
• ORDER BY and GROUP BY
• AND, OR, BETWEEN, IN, LIKE
• Joins
• Working with Dates
• Auto Increment
• Triggers
• Index & Views
• Commit & Rollback (transactions)
• Functions — MySQL built-in & User Defined
• SQL Queries — CRUD, Joins, Aggregations
• Database Indexing
• Integration with Spring Boot
